input.headline: US adds 119,000 jobs in September but unemployment hits four-year peak

raw.headline: US adds 119,000 jobs in September but unemployment hits four-year peak

winner: I got my first job ever—it’s called "Unemployment." The benefits are amazing, especially with the four-year peak bonus.

candidates.text: The economy added 119,000 jobs last month. Unfortunately, one of them was my motivation to stop applying for unemployment.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline. It uses a personal twist on unemployment in a clever way, making it both relatable and humorous."}

candidates.text: I heard the economy added 119,000 jobs last month... apparently, they're all doing "unemployment research" now!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.2, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and ties the headline's conflicting data points together with a clever twist."}

candidates.text: I got my first job ever—it’s called "Unemployment." The benefits are amazing, especially with the four-year peak bonus.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the headline's irony. It uses natural English and plays on the contradiction between job growth and unemployment, making it both funny and relevant."}

candidates.text: The economy added 119,000 new jobs last month, and suddenly, everyone was hired except one guy who just couldn't seem to fit in with the rest of the workforce—he kept trying to sit on the desk and share his snacks.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the imagery of someone trying to sit on the desk and share snacks, but it feels a bit forced and loosely connected to the headline. The humor lacks tight specificity and the surprise element is weak."}

candidates.text: I'm glad to hear that more people are getting back to work—finally, someone can afford those $5 coffee runs again!
